rules.reason: Stayful does not offer guaranteed rent. Saying this creates a liability and breaks trust when reality differs.
rules.alternative: Show historical monthly range. Emphasise payout reliability (1st-5th, never missed). Show floor not ceiling.

rules.reason: Overpromising occupancy is the single biggest trust-killer when the listing underperforms in month 1-3.
rules.alternative: Show conservative (65%) and realistic (70-75%) scenarios. Let the conservative case make the argument.

rules.reason: Attacking competitors by name damages Zac’s credibility. The performance data speaks for itself.
rules.alternative: 'I’ll let the numbers do the talking. How many reviews have you had in the last 3 months?' The gap becomes apparent without a word against the competitor.

rules.reason: Dismisses a genuine concern. Betrayal-damaged and certainty-seeker archetypes will disengage immediately.
rules.alternative: Name the concern specifically: 'You’re right to ask about [X]. Here’s how we handle it: [specific process].

rules.phrase: It’s really easy to manage / barely any work
rules.reason: Undermines the value of full management. If it’s easy, why pay 15%? Creates cognitive dissonance.
rules.alternative: 'Full management means you’re not doing any of it. Photography, pricing, guest comms, maintenance — that’s us. You see a bank transfer on the 1st.'

rules.reason: Sounds salesy. Effective Zac language is fact-based and direct, not persuasive or pushy.
rules.alternative: Present data. Let lead decide. Create urgency with deadline not pressure.

rules.reason: Implies previous statements weren’t honest. Avoid this construction entirely.
rules.alternative: Just be direct. No preamble needed.

rules.reason: Over-indexing on one platform. Stayful’s value is multi-platform + direct. Platform enthusiasm without mentioning direct bookings misses a key differentiator.
rules.alternative: 'We list on Airbnb and Booking.com, but 40% of our bookings come direct — no platform fee, better margin for you.'

rules.reason: Stayful manages ~70. Saying hundreds is inaccurate and contradicts the deliberate portfolio size argument.
rules.alternative: 'We manage around 70 properties. That’s deliberate — it means every owner has a named account manager and isn’t a ticket in a queue.'

rules.phrase: You’ll make more than your mortgage
rules.reason: Income promise. Only use specific figures from the analysis, never vague guarantees.
rules.alternative: 'Based on the analysis, you’re looking at £X net per month after bills. Your mortgage is £Y. That’s £Z above your costs.'
